patient_information.name: Maria Elena Rodriguez
patient_information.address: 4821 S Peoria Ave, Apt 304, Tulsa, OK 74105
patient_information.allergies: Penicillin (hives and shortness of breath as a teenager); seasonal allergies to ragweed causing nasal congestion and itchy eyes; no known allergies to IV iron products or contrast dyes documented to date.

physician_information.address_physician: 2100 S Utica Ave, Suite 450, Tulsa, OK 74114

injectafer_order.injectafer_order: Injectafer (ferric carboxymaltose) IV iron replacement therapy as per FDA labeling and institutional protocol.

diagnosis.other_diagnosis: Iron deficiency anemia secondary to chronic blood loss from heavy menstrual bleeding (menorrhagia) associated with uterine fibroids and probable adenomyosis; history of intolerance to oral iron supplementation due to significant gastrointestinal side effects (nausea, abdominal cramping, and constipation).
diagnosis.secondary_causal_diagnosis_code: N92.0 (Excessive and frequent menstruation with regular cycle); D25.9 (Leiomyoma of uterus, unspecified).

standing_lab_orders.frequency: CBC and CMP to be drawn at baseline on day of first infusion, then repeat CBC (including hemoglobin and hematocrit) 4 weeks after completion of the Injectafer course, and again at 3 months if clinically indicated. Abnormal results or symptomatic changes to be faxed to ordering provider within 24 hours.

notes_and_location.notes_additional_comments:
Patient is a 41-year-old premenopausal woman with a multi-year history of progressively worsening iron deficiency anemia related to heavy menstrual bleeding. She reports soaking through a super-plus tampon and pad every 1–2 hours on the first two days of her cycle, along with passing large clots and experiencing significant fatigue, shortness of breath when climbing stairs, and difficulty concentrating at work.

She has attempted multiple forms of oral iron supplementation over the past 18–24 months, including ferrous sulfate 325 mg once daily and then every other day, as well as a trial of a gentler polysaccharide iron complex. All were discontinued or poorly adhered to due to persistent nausea, abdominal cramping, constipation severe enough to require stool softeners, and dark stools that she found distressing. Despite these attempts, her hemoglobin has remained in the 9–10 g/dL range with ferritin consistently under 15 ng/mL (most recent ferritin 8 ng/mL, transferrin saturation 7%). She reports needing afternoon naps and has had to reduce her regular exercise and social activities because of fatigue.

Gynecology workup has identified uterine fibroids and probable adenomyosis; she is considering a levonorgestrel IUD placement to help manage bleeding but has not yet proceeded due to scheduling and childcare issues. In the meantime, hematology has been asked to optimize her iron stores and improve her functional status. She previously received a course of IV iron (not Injectafer) in August 2023 with a good hematologic response and significant improvement in symptoms for several months, but her anemia has since recurred as heavy menses have persisted.

She works full-time as a bilingual middle school teacher (English and Spanish) and is the primary caregiver for two young children, so minimizing time away from work and rapid symptom improvement are important to her. She has reliable transportation, no history of prior infusion reactions, and no known hypersensitivity to IV iron. She does, however, have a history of mild vasovagal episodes with blood draws, so please ensure the patient is seated or reclined and allow a few extra minutes post-infusion observation. She prefers to have detailed explanations of each step of the process and to be told in advance about any expected side effects such as temporary metallic taste, flushing, or changes in urine or stool color.

Please provide written after-visit instructions in English, and verbally review in both English and Spanish if staff are able, as she is fluent in both but sometimes prefers Spanish when discussing medical issues in detail. She has requested that appointment reminders be sent by both text and email when possible, as she juggles a busy school schedule.

If there are any issues with scheduling, pre-certification, or if she experiences concerning symptoms such as chest pain, difficulty breathing, swelling of the face or throat, or severe dizziness during or after infusion, please contact the ordering provider’s office immediately and provide same-day notification. For mild delayed reactions (e.g., arthralgias, myalgias, or transient flu-like symptoms), please advise her to contact the clinic nurse triage line for guidance. She is comfortable staying for the recommended observation period after each infusion.

Please coordinate infusion times around her work hours when feasible (late afternoon appointments are preferred). She is generally available after 3:30 PM on weekdays and has more flexibility on Fridays. Parking at the Tulsa site is manageable for her, and she is familiar with the location from prior visits.

Insurance pre-authorization is anticipated to be required due to prior IV iron use; documentation of failed oral iron therapy, ongoing menstrual blood loss, and current lab results has been provided with this referral to facilitate approval.
